is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study assesses mutations in dhfr and dhps genes in P. falciparum isolated from children with acute malaria in Guinea-Bissau.

reports_target_markers.evidence: Reports dhfr codons 51, 59, 108 and dhps codons 436, 437, 540 — all curated target codons.

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: Provides counts/percentages: 41% (triple dhfr mutation), 15% (15/97 triple+437), 52% codon 108 mutation, 29% dhps 437, 27% dhps 436-alanine, out of 97 PCR-analyzed samples; Figure 1 gives genotype counts.

sub_country_location.evidence: All samples from a single site: Bandim Health Centre in the semi-urban area of Bandim on the outskirts of Bissau — a sub-national location.

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: One hundred children were enrolled from January until May 2001 — collection window within a single year.

african_field_samples.evidence: Clinical isolates from children attending Bandim Health Centre in Bissau, Guinea-Bissau (African field samples).

is_primary_study.evidence: Primary empirical study with PCR/RFLP genotyping of collected field samples.

duplicate_risk.evidence: Stated as 'part of a malaria study (Kofoed PE et al., unpublished data)' but this is a routine field collection; no evidence of overlapping published molecular marker dataset.

supplement.evidence: Frequency data and single-site location are fully contained in main text and figures.

assessment.summary: Primary field study of P. falciparum from 97-100 children at Bandim Health Centre, Bissau, Guinea-Bissau (collected Jan-May 2001). Reports extractable frequencies for dhfr codons 51/59/108 and dhps codons 436/437/540 at a single sub-national site within a <=3-year window. Meets all eligibility criteria.
